channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Jeffries becomes highest-ranking Dem to call for Hegseth to resign
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries wrote a letter to President Donald Trump demanding that Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth be "fired immediately.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits the El Salvador prison where deported Venezuelans are held
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem on Wednesday will visit the high-security El Salvador prison where Venezuelans who the Trump administration alleges are members of the Venezuelan Tren de Aragua gang have been held since their removal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday. The team released a statement Tuesday afternoon that said it would visit the White House "in keeping with long-standing baseball tradition.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Michael Kratsios to lead White House science, tech office
item.description: Kratsios, who was Trump’s chief technology officer during his first White House term, was approved Tuesday by the upper chamber in a 74-25 vote.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Kansas has among the shortest windows for voting by mail in the US. It will get shorter next year
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Republican legislators in Kansas on Tuesday shrunk what already was among the nation's shortest windows for voting by mail, arguing that problems with the U.S. Postal Service's handling of ballots required the move. Critics called it voter suppression.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Dr Marty Makary as Trump's FDA chief
item.description: Fox News contributor Dr. Marty Makary was officially confirmed to lead the FDA in Trump's second administration after a vote on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's pick to lead NIH, Dr. Jay Bhattacharya, confirmed by Senate in party-line vote
item.description: The United States Senate gave its final stamp of approval for the nomination of Dr. Jay Bhattacharya as the next NIH director Tuesday evening.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Youngkin vetoes AI regulation legislation in Virginia
item.description: Youngkin said his administration and the state of Virginia already has established safeguards and oversight for AI use.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump defends Waltz after Signal breach
item.description: President Trump said he still has confidence in national security adviser Mike Waltz in an interview with NBC on Tuesday, following a report by a journalist for The Atlantic who says he was included in a text message chain with officials about plans for an attack on Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Eichorn tried to obstruct investigation and lied about having a gun, prosecutors say
item.description: Prosecutors are asking a judge to keep former Minnesota senator Justin Eichorn in jail, alleging he tried to keep them from accessing a computer and cell phone in his St. Paul apartment. They also alleged he lied about having a gun in the apartment.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Abortion once more plays a key role in a state political fight, this time in Wisconsin's court race
item.description: The winner of the April 1 election could hold the power to determine the fate of future litigation in Wisconsin over abortion because the outcome of the race will decide whether liberals or conservatives hold a majority on the state’s highest court.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: DHS revokes legal protections for 532,000 Cubans, Haitians, Nicaraguans and Venezuelans
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security's new policy, revoking legal protections for hundreds of thousands, impacts people who are already in the U.S. and who came under a humanitarian parole program.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota and six other states push to shift road funds to transit and bike projects as Trump threatens cuts
item.description: A seven-state group known as the Clean Rides Network gained momentum this year in its advocacy for environmentally friendly transportation projects that it says the federal government has abandoned.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Fate of Duluth lab unclear amid reports of possible closure of EPA’s research arm
item.description: More than 100 people work at the U.S. EPA lab in Duluth. It’s part of the agency’s Office of Research and Development, which the Trump administration is targeting for possible large-scale layoffs.
item.dc:creator: Dan Kraker, MPR News

item.title: Trump's plan to dismantle Education Dept. criticized by Nevada Dems
item.description: Nevada Democrats are criticizing President Donald Trump's effort to dismantle the US Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DFL, GOP educator-leaders respond to Trump‘s dismantling of the Education Department
item.description: Assistant Majority Leader and Chair of the Senate Education Finance Committee Sen. Mary Kunesh, DFL-New Brighton — who is a library media specialist — and House Education Policy Committee Co-Vice Chair Rep.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Gov. DeSantis, Border Czar Tom Homan discuss immigration enforcement in Sarasota
item.description: It’s no secret that immigration is a key political topic. That’s why it was featured as the subject matter on Thursday in New College of Florida’s The Socratic Stage.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks DOGE access to Social Security records
item.description: A federal judge in Maryland on Thursday temporarily blocked the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) from accessing millions of Americans’ personal data stored by the Social Security Administration (SSA). In a scathing, 137-page opinion, U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: White House: 'Critical functions' will stay under Department of Education
item.description: White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said all the critical functions of the Department of Education will remain ahead of President Trump signing an executive order to dismantle the federal agency.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans want Musk to shut up about Social Security
item.description: Senate Republicans want Elon Musk to stop talking about Social Security, and the Department of Government Efficiency to leave it alone.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers renew push to close unique Florida medical malpractice loophole
item.description: Tina Fitzgerald of Spring Hill is brought to tears thinking about her daughter who died after seeking medical attention.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Fed keeps rates unchanged as Trump tariffs, economic concerns loom
item.description: Despite some signs that the economy may be heading for a slowdown, the Federal Reserve is holding rates steady.
item.dc:creator: Tobias Burns, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Reba's Law takes aim at wide range of animal cruelty, giving prosecutors more tools
item.description: The horrific circumstances surrounding the death of Reba — the English bulldog that died after being abandoned in a sealed plastic tub left next to a dumpster on a hot Las Vegas day — proved to be the tipping point for prosecutors and the public.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
